news.title: Who's on the hook when an AI agent acts for you? Estonia wants to give agents their own di…
news.author: BenjaminHan@sigmoid.social
news.snippet: Who's on the hook when an AI agent acts for you? Estonia wants to give agents their own digital IDs, with scoped, per-action powers instead of a blanket handover of your rights. It can't make the software accountable, you can't fine or jail code, but it keeps the human behind the agent traceable. https:// benjaminhan.net/posts/20260620 -estonia-intends-to-recognize-ai-agents-with-di/?utm_source=mastodon&utm_medium=social # AIAgents # DigitalID # Policy # AI

news.title: Five independent reviewers comparing two new Chinese open models—GLM-5.2 and Kimi K2.7 Cod…
news.author: schuler
news.snippet: Five independent reviewers comparing two new Chinese open models—GLM-5.2 and Kimi K2.7 Code—found GLM-5.2 narrowly ahead on code quality and cost per task. Speed was mixed, but GLM's outputs held up better under inspection. Early results, not standardized benchmarks. https://www. implicator.ai/glm-5-2-edges-ki mi-k2-7-code-in-early-coding-tests/ # AI # OpenModels # CodingTools

news.title: How do you know whether a model's confidence scores can be trusted? It depends which metri…
news.author: BenjaminHan@sigmoid.social
news.snippet: How do you know whether a model's confidence scores can be trusted? It depends which metric you ask. Take three models that give the same answers and get the same number right, differing only in the confidence they report. ECE rewards the one that says 0.5 to everything. AUROC rewards the most overconfident one. Only a proper score like Brier or log loss picks the model you'd actually want. Optimize the wrong metric and you get a degenerate model. https:// benjaminhan.net/posts/20260609 -confidence-calibration-metrics/?utm_source=mastodon&utm_medium=social # AI # MachineLearning
